# datalogic-rs

> High-performance JSONLogic (json-logic) rules engine and sandboxed
> expression evaluator: one Rust core with official bindings for
> Node.js, browser/edge WASM, Python, Go, Java (JVM), .NET, and PHP,
> plus an embeddable React visual rule editor and step-through
> debugger. Business rules are plain JSON data: store them in a
> database, ship them over an API, change logic without a redeploy.
> Apache-2.0.

Key facts: rules compile once to opcode programs and evaluate in a
reusable memory arena (8.9 ns geomean across the 50-suite benchmark
matrix); the core is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` with no eval, no I/O,
and no scripting runtime, so user-submitted rules are safe to evaluate;
every binding runs the same 1,565-case conformance battery (54 suites)
in CI, so semantics never drift between languages; 59 built-in
operators including datetime, string/array extensions, error handling
(try/throw), and flagd-compatible feature-flag operators (fractional,
sem_ver) with murmur3 bucketing byte-compatible with OpenFeature flagd.

Install per language: Rust `cargo add datalogic-rs` · Node.js
`npm i @goplasmatic/datalogic-node` · Browser/Edge WASM
`npm i @goplasmatic/datalogic-wasm` · Python `pip install datalogic-py`
(fully typed, PEP 561) · Go
`go get github.com/GoPlasmatic/datalogic-rs/bindings/go/v5` · JVM
`io.github.goplasmatic:datalogic` (JDK 22+) · .NET
`dotnet add package Goplasmatic.Datalogic` · PHP
`composer require goplasmatic/datalogic` (PHP 8.4+, ext-ffi) · React UI
`npm i @goplasmatic/datalogic-ui`.

API shape (identical across languages): one-shot `apply(rule, data)`;
`Engine` → `compile(rule)` → `rule.evaluate(data)` for hot paths;
`Session` for arena-reuse loops; `DataHandle` for parse-once payloads;
typed results (bool/int/float) for predicates; batch evaluation;
custom operators registered as host-language callbacks; optional
templating mode and step-by-step trace output.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/introduction.html):
  what the engine is and when to use it
- [Quick start](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/getting-started/quick-start.html):
  first evaluation in every language
- [Operator reference](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/operators/overview.html):
  all 59 built-in operators with examples
- [Language guides](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/rust/overview.html):
  per-binding chapters (Rust, Node, WASM, Python, Go, JVM, .NET, PHP, React UI)
- [Security & sandboxing](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/advanced/security.html):
  the sandbox guarantees and what remains the host's job
- [How it compares](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/comparison.html):
  vs json-logic-js, json-logic-engine, GoRules ZEN, CEL
- [Performance](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/performance.html):
  benchmark methodology and numbers

## Try it

- [Live playground](https://goplasmatic.github.io/datalogic-rs/playground/):
  visual editor and step-through debugger running the real engine (WASM)
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/GoPlasmatic/datalogic-rs):
  source, examples for every language under bindings/<lang>/examples/
- [Rust API docs](https://docs.rs/datalogic-rs): docs.rs reference

## Notes for code generation

- Rule and data inputs: Node and Python accept native objects; WASM,
  Go, JVM, .NET, and PHP take JSON strings at the primary surface.
- Compile once and reuse the compiled rule; compiled rules are
  thread-safe in every binding. Sessions are single-threaded.
- Errors carry a stable tag (`ParseError`, `TypeMismatch`, `Thrown`,
  `NaN`, …) with the same taxonomy in every language.
- The old v4 npm package `@goplasmatic/datalogic` no longer exists on
  the registry; use `@goplasmatic/datalogic-node` (native Node) or
  `@goplasmatic/datalogic-wasm` (browser/edge).
